sheets.name: README
rows: Maps user-facing onboarding responses into backend profile fields, persona IDs, overlays, class affinity vectors, confidence scores, and add-on flags.
Generate weekly meal classes before dish recommendations.
Keep primary family meals separate from member-specific add-ons.
Do not invent new IDs in application code; manage changes through DOC-27/DOC-28.
Apply hard constraints before scoring and personalization.

sheets.name: Main_Cohort_Hierarchy
rows: Single, student, professional, flatmate, migrant adult
Show 5-6 sub-options: student/hostel, young professional, working woman alone, flatmates, migrant adult, desk/fitness adult
Then ask cooking system, diet/nonveg, health/fitness, origin-current city.
DINK, newly married, mixed-state couple, trying/pregnant/new parents
Show DINK, newly married, mixed-state, planning pregnancy, pregnant household, infant/baby household
Then ask baby age if selected, food restrictions, nonveg frequency, cook availability, origin-current city.
Toddler, school kid, teen, picky child, family budget/value, homemaker family
Show toddler, school kid, teen, picky eater, family budget, homemaker elaborate
Children-specific food becomes add-on component; main plan remains family meal.
Joint family, elderly couple, recovery/senior, elder + child, chronic condition in family
Show joint family, elderly couple, recovery light, elder+child, diabetic/BP member
Elder/recovery food becomes add-on component unless the whole household follows it.
Special goal or kitchen operating mode
Health/fitness, Jain/fasting, nonveg mode, cook-assisted, maid-dependent, premium foodie
Show health, gym/protein, vegetarian protein, Jain, fasting, skilled cook, cook-needs-instruction, maid-dependent, regular nonveg
This can be selected first or captured as overlay after MC1-MC4. Do not force user to browse all detailed personas.

sheets.name: Routing_Rules_v3
rows: Select one of 5: Just me, Couple, Family with children, Joint/elders, Special goal/kitchen mode
Narrows to 5-16 sub options without showing 41 personas.
Show only sub-cohorts under selected main cohort; e.g., Family with children -> toddler/school kid/teen/picky child/budget/homemaker.
Ask “Do they need a separate soft/mild/extra component?” default yes.
Creates add-on component plan, not main meal class.
If health selected OR family member has diabetes/BP/weight goal
Ask “Is this for everyone or only one member?”
If one member, use swap/add-on; if whole household, health classes can enter main rotation.
Self-cook, skilled cook, cook needs instructions, maid/helper, tiffin/PG, delivery-heavy.
Adjusts complexity, prep batching, instruction detail.
Veg, egg, nonveg, Jain, vegan, allergies; for nonveg ask weekly frequency + proteins eaten.
Sets hard constraints and weekly protein schedule.
Where are you from and where do you live now?
Blends home-state signature with current city lifestyle overlay.
Swipe meal classes, not dishes: Simple sabzi, dal-rice, paratha, egg breakfast, fish curry rice, etc.

sheets.name: Persona_Lookup
rows: budget, repeat tolerant; bread/poha breakfast; one-pot or outside dinner
MC1 > SC1A; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
weekday quick meals, office lunch, dinner light or delivery
MC1 > SC1B; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
healthy intent but time-starved; predictable quick classes
MC1 > SC1C; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
weekday quick; weekend experimental or outside
MC2 > SC2A; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
alternates two home-state signatures; high discovery need
MC2 > SC2B; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
protein, iron, fruits, lower outside food
MC2 > SC2C; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
balanced mild meals, aversion-aware, iron/protein focus
MC2 > SC2D; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
adult meals quick; infant milk; mother supportive meals
MC2 > SC2E; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
adult meal plus soft baby add-on; low spice batch cook
MC2 > SC2F; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
mild, soft, routine, toddler spillover plate
MC3 > SC3A; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
tiffin plus adult dinner, mild but not baby food
MC3 > SC3B; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
higher appetite, protein, less repeat tolerance
MC3 > SC3C; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
multi-dish meals, elderly and child needs, high coherence
MC4 > SC4A; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
early dinner, soft, low oil, stable routines
MC4 > SC4B; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
low GI, portioned carbs, protein pairing
MC4 > SC4D; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
low salt/oil, steamed/boiled, fewer fried snacks
MC4 > SC4E; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
salads, chilla, low-carb dinner, controlled indulgence
MC5 > SC5A; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
MC5 > SC5B; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
paneer, soy, dal, sprouts; avoids empty carb plate
MC5 > SC5C; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
MC5 > SC5D; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
MC5 > SC5E; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
can execute multi-dish regional and weekend specials
MC5 > SC5F; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
simple repeatable classes, avoid complex regional until trained
MC5 > SC5G; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
cook-instruction plan, prep delegation, lunchbox predictability
MC5 > SC5H; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
batch dal/sabzi, one-pot, leftovers, avoid precision dishes
MC5 > SC5I; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
seasonal vegetables, dal, eggs if eaten, low delivery
MC3 > SC3E; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
regional discovery, high weekend novelty, lower repeat tolerance
MC5 > SC5J; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
Migrant in metro preserving home-state food
home-state staples mixed with current-city lifestyle
MC1 > SC1E; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
dual-state rotations across week, weekend swaps
MC2 > SC2G; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
nonveg 3-6 meals/week depending state; weekday egg/chicken, weekend special
MC5 > SC5K; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
eggs accepted, meat rare/outside, protein bridge
MC5 > SC5L; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
fish rice and fish fry frequent; seafood weekend
MC5 > SC5M; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
weekday veg/egg/chicken, Sunday mutton or biryani
MC5 > SC5N; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
home remains veg; nonveg appears outside/delivery weekend
MC5 > SC5O; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
child-safe defaults and adult vegetables hidden/rotated
MC3 > SC3D; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
soft protein, khichdi, soups, low spice, frequent small meals
MC4 > SC4C; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
shared staples, split groceries, low repeat tolerance, one-pot
MC1 > SC1D; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
heavier breakfast, late lunch, robust dinner
MC5 > SC5P; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
light lunch/dinner, snack control, protein and greens
MC1 > SC1F; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
more multi-dish meals and regional specials; weekday home food strong
MC3 > SC3F; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
Family main meal is normal dal/roti/rice/sabzi; child/elder/diabetic needs are handled as add-on components and swaps, not as primary meal replacement.
MC4 > SC4F; ask overlays: diet/nonveg, cook system, health/lifecycle, origin-current city
